kerf
Claim founding seat

Stop paying monthly
to cut plywood.

One-time $49, yours forever. No subscription. Ever.

Founding price — first 300 seats (real counter), then $99.99.

Get lifetime access — $49

Price includes VAT where applicable · 14-day no-questions refund · By purchasing you consent to immediate digital delivery and waive the EU withdrawal right after download.

How it works

  1. Enter your cut list. Parts, quantities, grain direction. Metric or fractional inches — 10 parts in under a minute.
  2. Get the optimal plan. Guillotine-safe layouts your panel saw can actually cut, kerf and trim accounted for, deterministic every time.
  3. Cut in workshop mode. Big type, screen stays awake, tap each cut as you go. Sawdust-friendly.

Why not the web-based subscription optimizers?

Subscription optimizersKerf
Price$5–15 every month, foreverOne-time $49 founding ($99.99 after)
Works offline in the shopMostly no — browser requiredYes — fully offline, your data stays on device
Workshop mode at the sawNoBig-type step-by-step cut list
2D panels + 1D linear stockUsually separate toolsBoth, one app
Cloud sync across devicesYes — their strong suitv1: on-device (iCloud sync on the roadmap)

Honest table: sync is genuinely their advantage today. We'd rather ship offline-first and add sync than the reverse.

Yearly

$39.99/year

  • Everything while active
  • Renewal date always visible

Questions, answered straight

What if it's not for me?
14-day refund, no questions asked. Email us, money back.
Which platforms?
iOS first (launching now), Android next, web lite calculator free in your browser. Founding license covers the platform you buy on — pricing parity promise across platforms.
Does it work offline?
Completely. Your cut lists never leave your device. No account required.
Is the counter real?
Yes — it reads live sales data. No fake countdowns, no fake scarcity. When 300 seats are gone, the price is $99.99.
Subscription trap?
Lifetime means lifetime. The yearly and weekend options exist for people who prefer them — the weekend pass never auto-renews.
